seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Turton and Polish Hill River. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Turton vs Polish Hill River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Turton and Polish Hill River.
heading: Point Turton vs Polish Hill River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Turton, South Australia and Polish Hill River, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Turton has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Polish Hill River (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Turton or Polish Hill River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Turton has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Polish Hill River scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Turton scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Polish Hill River scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Turton or Polish Hill River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Turton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
